recent_decisions.title: Approve £850k underspend allocation and £68k debt write-off
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council approved allocating £850k underspend (£250k to Capital/Repairs, £600k for reorganisation costs) and writing off £68k debt. No votes recorded.

recent_decisions.title: Approve £63k capital budget reduction and £157k increase for hockey pitch
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council approved £63k reduction in Technology One Finance System costs and £157k increase for hockey pitch at Buckshaw Village. No votes recorded.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Capital budget increase for hockey pitch
key_data_points: £63k reduction in Technology One Finance System

recent_decisions.title: Approve contractor for All Seasons Leisure Centre improvements
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council approved appointing John Turner Construction Ltd for improvements and decarbonisation at All Seasons Leisure Centre. No votes recorded.

recent_decisions.title: Exclude public from confidential item
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council voted to exclude public and press from confidential item on All Seasons Leisure Centre contract. No votes recorded.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of minutes from previous meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The minutes from the November 2025 meeting were unanimously approved as an accurate record.

motions.text: Resolved (unanimously) that the minutes of the meeting held on 26 November 2025 be approved as an accurate record for signing by the Chair.

recent_decisions.title: Audit findings report 2024-25 noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The audit findings report was noted, with members thanking auditors for their clear report and positive overall picture.

motions.text: Resolved that the report be noted.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of amendments to Statement of Accounts 2024/25
recent_decisions.political_summary: Amendments to the Statement of Accounts, including the valuation of Astley Hall, were unanimously approved.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Valuation of Astley Hall and other minor changes

motions.text: Resolved (unanimously) to approve the amendments to the Statement of Accounts for 2024/25 (Appendix 1), as identified by Grant Thornton during the final stages of the completion of the external audit.

recent_decisions.title: Internal Audit Progress Report noted and Repairs and Maintenance Review deferred
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Internal Audit Progress Report was noted, and the Repairs and Maintenance Review was deferred to allow new processes to embed.

motions.text: Resolved (unanimously) 1. To note the position with regard to the Internal Audit Plan and management actions. 2. To agree the deferral of the Repairs and Maintenance Review for the reasons provided.

recent_decisions.title: Audit and Governance Committee Effectiveness Review noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Audit and Governance Committee Effectiveness Review was noted, with one action for further development identified.
key_data_points: One action for further development identified
Training and development programme to be presented next meeting

motions.text: Resolved that the report be noted.

recent_decisions.title: Approved planning application for Charter Lane with S106 contributions
recent_decisions.political_summary: Planning permission granted for Charter Lane development with £262,300 in S106 contributions (£40k/year for bus service, £182,400 for open space, £39,900 if private maintenance not used). Vote: 6 for, 3 against.
recent_decisions.financial_context: S106 contributions for bus service, open space, and affordable housing
key_data_points: £40,000 per annum for bus service for 3 years
Additional £39,900 if private maintenance not used

motions.text: Proposed to refuse the planning application.
Proposed to grant planning permission subject to conditions and S106 agreement.

recent_decisions.title: Refused planning in principle for Land off Mill Lane
recent_decisions.political_summary: Planning in principle refused for Land off Mill Lane due to open space concerns. Vote: 7 for, 2 against.
key_data_points: Contrary to local and national planning policies

motions.text: Proposed to refuse planning in principle due to open space concerns.

recent_decisions.title: Granted advertisement consent for Millennium Way
recent_decisions.political_summary: Advertisement consent granted for Millennium Way for 12 months. Vote: 6 for, 2 against, 1 abstain.

motions.text: Proposed to grant advertisement consent for a temporary period of 12 months.

recent_decisions.title: Confirmed Tree Preservation Order 2025 No 4
recent_decisions.political_summary: Tree Preservation Order formally confirmed unanimously.

recent_decisions.title: Noted report on HMO Article 4 Direction
recent_decisions.political_summary: Report on HMO Article 4 Direction noted by the committee.

recent_decisions.title: Noted Appeals Report
recent_decisions.political_summary: Appeals Report noted by the committee.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of minutes from previous meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The minutes from the October 2025 meeting were unanimously approved as a correct record.

motions.text: That the minutes of the meeting held on Thursday, 2 October 2025 be agreed as a correct record.

recent_decisions.title: Request for business grant beneficiary data
recent_decisions.political_summary: The panel requested data on businesses benefiting from grants, with no vote recorded.

motions.text: That information be provided to the Panel regarding the number of businesses which had benefited from the business grants.

recent_decisions.title: Improved performance indicator graphs
recent_decisions.political_summary: Future reports will include details of projects/indicators alongside summary graphs.

motions.text: That regarding the summary graphs for the performance indicators, it was agreed that future reports will include details of the projects/indicators to which they refer, alongside the graphic.

recent_decisions.title: Request for customer satisfaction comparison
recent_decisions.political_summary: The panel requested comparative customer satisfaction data from South Ribble Borough Council.

motions.text: That comparative information be provided from South Ribble Borough Council, regarding the % of customers satisfied with the service received.

recent_decisions.title: Approved minutes of November 2025 meeting with amendment
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council unanimously approved minutes from November 2025 with a minor amendment to clarify a vote on local government reorganisation.

motions.text: Resolved (unanimously) that the minutes of the Council meeting held on 18 November 2025 be approved as a correct record for signature by the Mayor, subject to an amendment of the word 'acknowledged' to ‘explained' on minute number 51 Local Government Reorganisation Proposal paragraph seven to read 'The Leader of the Opposition, Councillor Alan Platt requested the vote be split into two sections, but it was explained that the recommendations were linked and formed one decision'.

recent_decisions.title: Noted Executive Cabinet reports from 2025
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council unanimously noted reports from Executive Cabinet meetings held in 2025.

motions.text: Members considered general reports of the Executive Cabinet meetings held on 16 October, 27 November 11 December and 22 January. The Leader, Councillor Alistair Bradley proposed and the Deputy Leader, Councillor Peter Wilson seconded the report, and it was resolved (unanimously) that the report be noted.

recent_decisions.title: Noted Overview and Scrutiny Committee reports
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council unanimously noted reports from Overview and Scrutiny Committee and Task and Finish Groups.

motions.proposer: Councillor Debra Platt (Chair of Overview and Scrutiny Committee)
motions.seconder: Councillor Arjun Singh (Vice Chair of Overview and Scrutiny Committee)
motions.text: Members considered a general report of the Overview and Scrutiny Committee meeting held on 23 October, the Overview and Scrutiny Performance Panel meetings held on 2 October and 4 December and Task Group update. The Chair of the Overview and Scrutiny Committee, Councillor Debra Platt proposed and the Vice Chair of the Overview and Scrutiny Committee, Councillor Arjun Singh seconded the report, and it was resolved (unanimously) that the report be noted.

recent_decisions.title: Noted Audit and Governance Committee reports
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council unanimously noted reports from Audit and Governance Committee meetings.

motions.proposer: Councillor Alan Platt (Chair of Audit and Governance Committee)
motions.seconder: Councillor Mark Clifford (Vice Chair of Audit and Governance Committee)
motions.text: Members considered a general report of the Governance Committee meetings held on 24 September and 26 November. The Chair of the Audit and Governance Committee, Councillor Alan Platt proposed and the Vice Chair of the Audit and Governance Committee, Councillor Mark Clifford seconded the report, and it was resolved (unanimously) that the report be noted.

recent_decisions.title: Approved polling station changes in Chorley North East Ward
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council unanimously approved no change to St Barnabas Church polling station and relocated another in Chorley North East Ward.

motions.text: The Deputy Leader, Councillor Peter Wilson proposed, and the Executive Leader, Councillor Alistair Bradley seconded, and it was resolved (unanimously) 1. To approve no change to the polling station from St Barnabas Church, Chapel Lane, Heapey for the residents of Ryecroft and Ryefields in the Chorley North East ward. 2. To approve the relocation of the polling station in Chorley North East Ward from a classroom at St Joseph’s Catholic School, Bury Lane, Withnell, to a welfare unit sited on the s

recent_decisions.title: Noted responses to Article 4 Direction consultation
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council unanimously noted responses to Article 4 Direction consultation, with 7 in support and 4 against.

motions.seconder: Councillor Alex Hilton (Executive Member for Planning and Development)
motions.text: The Deputy Leader, Councillor Peter Wilson proposed, and the Executive Member, (Planning and Development), Councillor Alex Hilton seconded, and it was resolved (unanimously) 1. To note the responses received to the public consultation following the making of the immediate Article 4 Direction.

recent_decisions.committee: Overview and Scrutiny Task Group - Understanding and supporting people with Neurodiversity
recent_decisions.title: Appointed Chair and Vice Chair for Task Group
recent_decisions.political_summary: Councillor Russ Green was unanimously appointed Chair and Councillor Michelle Beach Vice Chair of the Task Group.

motions.text: That Councillor Russ Green be appointed Chair of this Task Group; and That Councillor Michelle Beach be appointed Vice Chair.

recent_decisions.committee: Overview and Scrutiny Task Group - Understanding and supporting people with Neurodiversity
recent_decisions.title: Scoping Review on Disability Access
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Task Group agreed to focus on neurodiversity, physical accessibility, and support groups, with further discussions planned.
key_data_points: Focus on neurodiversity, physical accessibility, and support groups

motions.text: That the presentation and notes of this discussion be circulated by email; That members consider further suggestions / ideas, and email them to Clare Gornall in Democratic Services to facilitate an online “brainstorming” exercise; That the next meeting be held on Monday, 23 February 2026 at 5.45pm to refine and complete the scoping process, and to agree the next steps for this review.

recent_decisions.title: Awarded 5-year water contract to Water Plus for £180k/year
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council approved a 5-year water contract with Water Plus for £180,000/year (excluding VAT), with annual CPI increases. The decision was unanimous.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Annual cost for water and wastewater services
key_data_points: Single bid received from Water Plus

motions.text: To authorise the award of a five-year contract for water and wastewater services for the Councils operational properties to Water Plus, effective from January 2026. The annual contract value is approximately £180,000 (excluding VAT), subject to yearly adjustments in accordance with the Consumer Price Index.

recent_decisions.title: Approved 2026/27 budget with 2.99% council tax rise
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council approved a 2.99% council tax increase for 2026/27, balancing the budget while noting a £0.73m shortfall in 2027/28.
key_data_points: Second lowest council tax in Lancashire

motions.text: That Executive Cabinet agree the contents of this report in order to start the budget consultation process as follows: A proposed 2.99% increase in council tax in 2026/27; A forecast balanced budget for 2026/27 (based on a 2.99% increase in council tax, as noted above); A forecast budget shortfall for 2027/28 of £0.730m, to be addressed through the development and delivery of the council’s Fit for the Future transformation programme alongside the potential application of reserves held specifical

recent_decisions.title: Approved 2026/27 fees and charges increases
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council approved various fee increases including 3.8% for leisure services, 4.8% for council housing rents, and adjustments to service charges.
key_data_points: £1/sq ft increase for Strawberry Fields service charge
3.5% increase in Cotswold House service charge

motions.text: To approve an increase in Leisure fees and charges of 3.8% in relation to direct leisure charges in line with September CPI (rounded up to nearest 5p) or 4.5% for hire and food and beverage charges as per point 18

To approve an uplift in the rents of all Council owned housing (including Extra Care properties, but excluding Cotswold House) of 4.8%. This is the approach agreed by Executive Cabinet in July 2022 of increasing these rents by CPI plus 1%, with CPI based on the published rate for September each year.

recent_decisions.title: Approved £157k increase for hockey pitch at Buckshaw Village
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet approved £157k increase for hockey pitch at Buckshaw Village, reducing £63k from shared finance system costs. No vote recorded.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Play, Recreation and Open Space Projects

recent_decisions.title: Noted revised Capital Programme and Balance Sheet position
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet noted revised Capital Programme and Balance Sheet position as of 31 December 2025, including £0.388m amendment. No vote recorded.

recent_decisions.title: Noted Corporate Strategy performance report
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet noted 21 Corporate Strategy projects: 6 complete, 13 on track, 2 delayed. Food waste collection delays resolved. No vote recorded.

recent_decisions.title: Excluded public and press for confidential item
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet resolved to exclude public and press for confidential item under Local Government Act 1972. No vote recorded.
